ddls:
CREATE TABLE "borrowers" (
  "borrower_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each borrower */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* The first name of the borrower */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* The last name of the borrower */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* The date of birth of the borrower */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* The phone number of the borrower */,
  "email" TEXT /* The email address of the borrower */,
  "address" TEXT /* The full address of the borrower */,
  "emp_title" TEXT /* The employment title of the borrower */,
  "home_ownership" INTEGER /* The home ownership status of the borrower (e.g., 0 - RENT, 1 - MORTGAGE, 2 - OWN) */,
  "annual_inc" INTEGER /* The annual income of the borrower */,
  "verification_status" INTEGER /* The verification status of the borrower's income (e.g., 0 - Not Verified, 1 - Verified) */,
  "credit_score" INTEGER /* The credit score of the borrower */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* The current employment status of the borrower (e.g., employed, self-employed, unemployed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("borrower_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "loan_officers" (
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each loan officer */,
  "name" TEXT /* The name of the loan officer */,
  "email" TEXT /* The email address of the loan officer */,
  "department" TEXT /* The department the loan officer belongs to */,
  "position" TEXT /* The position of the loan officer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* The phone number of the loan officer */,
  "date_hired" TEXT /* The date the loan officer was hired */,
  "status" TEXT /* The current status of the loan officer (e.g., active, on leave, terminated) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "loan_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each loan status */,
  "loan_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the loan */,
  "status" INTEGER /* The status of the loan (e.g., 0 - Current, 1 - Fully Paid, 2 - Late, etc.) */,
  "status_date" TEXT /* The date the loan status was updated */,
  "status_reason" TEXT /* The reason for the current status (e.g., paid in full, default, foreclosure) */,
  "status_updated_by" INTEGER /* The ID of the user who updated the status */,
  "status_notes" TEXT /* Any notes or comments regarding the status change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_loan_status_status_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("status_updated_by") REFERENCES loan_officers ("officer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payments" (
  "payment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payment */,
  "loan_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the loan */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* The date the payment was made */,
  "payment_amount" TEXT /* The amount of the payment */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* The method of payment (e.g., bank transfer, check, online payment) */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* The status of the payment (e.g., pending, completed, failed) */,
  "late_fee" TEXT /* The late fee amount if applicable */,
  "payment_reference" TEXT /* A reference number for the payment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "loan_types" (
  "loan_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each loan type */,
  "loan_type_name" TEXT /* The name of the loan type (e.g., Personal, Mortgage, Credit Card) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A description of the loan type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("loan_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "loan_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each loan version */,
  "loan_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the loan */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* The version number */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* The date the version was created */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Any notes or comments regarding the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* The name of the user role (e.g., Loan Officer, Credit Analyst, Administrator) */,
  "description" TEXT /* A description of the user role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the user role */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* The access level (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES loan_officers ("officer_id")
)
